sections.html:
Art Movement · 1920s–1930s

Art Deco

The distinctive visual language of Art Deco — combining elegant, geometric, angular elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value elegant and geometric design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.
